lessons.title: Learn: Command Line Basics and Text Handling
lessons.content_md:
# Lab 02: Command Line Basics

This lab teaches you how to create output, inspect files, filter text, and combine commands with pipes/redirection.

## Learning Objectives

- Understand command output and terminal streams
- Use `echo`, `cat`, `head`, `tail`, and `wc` for text operations
- Use `grep` for filtering and searching text
- Use pipes (`|`) and redirects (`>`, `>>`) safely

## Core Topics

### 1) Output and Redirection

```bash
echo 'Hello'
echo 'line one' > notes.txt
echo 'line two' >> notes.txt
```

- `>` overwrites file content
- `>>` appends to existing content

### 2) Reading Files

```bash
cat notes.txt
head -n 3 notes.txt
tail -n 3 notes.txt
```

### 3) Counting and Searching

```bash
wc -l notes.txt
wc -w notes.txt
grep 'line' notes.txt
```

### 4) Pipes

```bash
cat notes.txt | grep line
cat notes.txt | wc -l
```

Pipes connect commands so output from one command becomes input to another.

## Safety Notes

- Verify file path before using `>` because it can overwrite data
- Prefer read-only commands first (`cat`, `head`, `tail`)
- Use `history` to inspect what you ran

After this Learn section, complete guided command practice, exercises, and MCQ quiz.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 1 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 1

Run these commands to build your base text workspace.

```bash
cd ~ && pwd
mkdir -p ~/lab2
echo 'B-HackMe command lab'
echo 'line one' > ~/lab2/notes.txt
echo 'line two' >> ~/lab2/notes.txt
cat ~/lab2/notes.txt
head -n 1 ~/lab2/notes.txt
tail -n 1 ~/lab2/notes.txt
wc -l ~/lab2/notes.txt
wc -w ~/lab2/notes.txt
```

tasks.description: C01: Go to home and print working directory.
C02: Create directory lab2 in home.
C03: Print B-HackMe command lab using echo.
C04: Write line one to notes.txt using overwrite redirection.
C05: Append line two to notes.txt.
C06: Print full content of notes.txt.
C07: Show only the first line of notes.txt.
C08: Show only the last line of notes.txt.
C09: Count lines in notes.txt.
C10: Count words in notes.txt.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 2 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 2

Now practice grep, pipes, and command history.

```bash
grep 'line' ~/lab2/notes.txt
grep 'two' ~/lab2/notes.txt
cat ~/lab2/notes.txt | grep line
cat ~/lab2/notes.txt | wc -l
printf 'alpha\nbeta\ngamma\n' > ~/lab2/list.txt
cat ~/lab2/list.txt
head -n 2 ~/lab2/list.txt
tail -n 2 ~/lab2/list.txt
wc -c ~/lab2/list.txt
history | tail -n 10
```

tasks.description: C11: Search for the word line in notes.txt.
C12: Search for the word two in notes.txt.
C13: Use a pipe to pass notes.txt content into grep line.
C14: Use a pipe to count lines in notes.txt.
C15: Create list.txt with alpha, beta, gamma lines.
C16: Print full content of list.txt.
C17: Show first two lines from list.txt.
C18: Show last two lines from list.txt.
C19: Count characters in list.txt using wc -c.
C20: Show recent command history.

lessons.title: Exercises (5)
lessons.content_md:
## Practical Exercises

Complete all 5 exercises using your command-line basics skills.

- E1-E2: easy
- E3-E4: medium
- E5: medium-hard

tasks.description: E1: Create file report.txt inside lab2 with three lines: red, blue, green.
E2: Count lines in report.txt.
E3: Find the line containing blue from report.txt.
E4: Use a pipe to count words in report.txt.
E5: Append done to report.txt and confirm with tail -n 1.

lessons.title: MCQ Quiz (5)
lessons.content_md:
## MCQ: Command Line Basics

Answer using option index (0-3).

Example: if option 2 is correct, submit `2`.

tasks.description: Q1: Which command prints text to terminal output?
Q2: Which symbol appends output to an existing file?
Q3: Which command shows only the first lines of a file?
Q4: Which command is best to search text patterns in files?
Q5: In `cat file | wc -l`, what does `|` do?
tasks.validation_rule: ["Deletes output","Passes output to next command","Renames file","Compresses file"]
